attacking_style: Operates from breakaways rather than bunch sprints, capable of winning from small group selections after extended distances (156km+). Exploits tactical miscalculations by sprint teams.
team_tactics: Uses sprinter reputation as tactical deception—allows peloton to underestimate breakaway threat while preserving energy for final effort rather than leading out.
terrain_preference: Long, attritional stages that thin breakaway groups to 3-4 riders where sprint speed becomes decisive advantage over pure breakaway specialists.
vulnerability: Single race observation limits profile—unclear how performs when sprinters' teams control effectively or in pure bunch sprint scenarios.
key_patterns: Won from 4-man breakaway when given excessive freedom by sprinters' teams (1:11 gap).
Faster than typical breakaway riders in small group sprints despite domestique role expectations.
Benefits from tactical misreads where teams underestimate threat based on perceived lead-out duties.
form_trajectory: unknown: Single victory demonstrates opportunistic quality and tactical intelligence, but insufficient data to establish trajectory trend
